texto: ... an impression of inescapable noise or acute disorder, a rush of adrenalin, sensations of alarm, a sense of unbalance or chaos, residual feeling of nausea and anxiety. These are the forms of bodily distress that occur when one's ingrained, taken-for-granted sense of how certain things are - and thus presumably will be and in some sense should be - is suddenly or insistently confronted by something very much at odds with it.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Barbara Herrnstein Smith sobre temas ligados a saude mental. A afirmação “... an impression of inescapable noise or acute disorder, a rush of adrenalin, sensations of alar…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a saude-mental, belief, denial, útil em momentos de dia-chuvoso e denial.
